hypothesis: Concrete eBPF Bytecode Modification:** Implement an eBPF memory monitoring script using performance monitoring counters (PMCs) to track L1 cache misses. Inject custom eBPF bytecode that acts as an asynchronous prefetcher during the sorting routine. The bytecode utilizes non-blocking `bpf_probe_read_user` instructions to force the memory controller to fetch the next cache-aligned data block ($B_{i+1}$) into the L1 cache concurrently while the primary CPU thread executes SIMD block moves (`vmovdqa` equivalents) on the current block ($B_i$). This ensures execution units remain saturated and memory access latency is hidden behind the asynchronous snapshot execution.
